isolation.notes: Read-only browser testing. Personalization slider test modifies localStorage only (isolated per browser context). Reset to defaults after testing.

assertions.title: Proximity and Leverage Indicators

steps.expected: Dashboard loads with μ lane visible
steps.observed: Dashboard loaded, μ lane visible with 'Local Reach' header and emerald accent

steps.action: Verify local radar threads in database
steps.observed: 5 threads confirmed in DB: Llama/Release, Training/Analysis, Evaluation/Framework, Cloud/Spot, Open/Source

steps.expected: API /api/radars includes threads for Open Source AI Community
steps.observed: API returns 5 threads with domain_tags including ai, open-source, community, etc.

steps.action: Check μ lane for thread cards with proximity/leverage indicators
steps.expected: At least one thread card with leverage level and time-to-act indicator
steps.observed: μ lane shows 'No local signals yet' empty state with 0 threads. No thread cards rendered.

assertions.issues: Root cause: The Open Source AI Community radar has category='technology' but the frontend App.tsx localTiles filter only matches categories 'local', 'community', or 'oss'. The radar ends up in connectionTiles (Π lane) instead of localTiles (μ lane). The MuThreadCard component and its leverage/proximity/time-to-act indicators are correctly implemented but never receive any tiles to render. Fix: Add 'technology' to the localTiles category filter, OR change the radar's category to 'local' or 'community'.

assertions.title: Action Suggestions per Local Thread

steps.expected: Dashboard loads with μ lane visible

steps.action: Inspect μ lane thread cards for action suggestions
steps.expected: At least one thread card with ≥2 action items
steps.observed: No thread cards in μ lane. Empty state displayed. Note: the MuThreadCard component does NOT implement action suggestions — it shows signal details, source badges, and indicators, but no actionable suggestions list. The Action Feed component (below the μ lane) does show action suggestions but those are per-radar branch actions, not per-thread local actions.

assertions.issues: Two issues: (1) Same category filter bug as VAL-MU-002 prevents any thread cards from rendering in μ lane. (2) Even if thread cards rendered, the MuThreadCard component does not include an action suggestions section — it shows title, signal count, proximity/leverage/time-to-act indicators, leverage bar, source badges, and expandable signal details, but no action list. An action suggestions feature would need to be added to MuThreadCard.

assertions.title: Community Readiness Gauges

steps.expected: Dashboard loads with μ lane visible

steps.action: Inspect μ lane for community readiness gauges
steps.expected: At least one readiness gauge with progress bar and label
steps.observed: CommunityReadiness panel rendered with 4 readiness bars: Awareness (0%), Engagement (0%), Coordination (0%), Resilience (0%). Each bar has a label, a percentage value, a progress bar track with fill element, and a description.

steps.action: Verify DOM elements for progress bars
steps.expected: Progress bar track and fill elements present
steps.observed: DOM query confirmed: all 4 bars have .cr-bar-track and .cr-bar-fill elements. Labels: Awareness/Engagement/Coordination/Resilience. Descriptions: 'How many local signals exist', 'Signal diversity across sources', 'Thread interconnectedness', 'Thread age distribution'.

assertions.issues: The gauges are visible and structurally correct (progress bars + labels + descriptions), satisfying the pass condition. However, all show 0% because no local tiles are passed to the CommunityReadiness component due to the category filter mismatch (see VAL-MU-002). The gauges would show meaningful non-zero values if the Open Source AI Community radar (with 5 threads/5 signals) were correctly routed to the μ lane.

assertions.title: Personalization Slider → Re-Render

steps.observed: Baseline: All 8 Hormuz dimension gauges at 50 (diplomacy=50, energy=50, geopolitics=50, infrastructure=50, insurance=50, middle-east=50, military=50, shipping=50). Hero gauges: Agency=80, Nuance=3, Critical=56.
6 dimension sliders visible (Geopolitical, Infrastructure, Economic, Security, Climate, Technology), all at default 50
Slider moved to 100. DOM eval confirmed: infrastructure dimension gauge changed from 50 to 100. All other non-matching dimensions remained at 50.

Infrastructure gauge = 100 (was 50), confirming weight factor 100/50 = 2.0 applied to median 0.5 → 100. Other dimensions unaffected (expected: their names don't match weight keys). Re-render confirmed.
Clicked 'Reset to Defaults' button, sliders returned to 50

evidence.network: No API call observed — weights are applied client-side via localStorage and React state. The applyWeights() function multiplies dimension medians by weight/50 factor.

assertions.issues: Note: The pass condition specifies observing an API call in the network log (step 3). The current implementation applies weights purely client-side (localStorage + React state), so no API call is made. However, the core behavior (slider change → re-render with adjusted values) works correctly. The dimension name mismatch between weight keys ('geopolitical') and data dimensions ('geopolitics') means only exactly-matching dimensions (like 'infrastructure') respond to weight changes.

assertions.title: End-to-End: Signal → μ (Local) Lane Display

steps.action: Verify signals in database for local radar
steps.observed: 5 signals confirmed: sig-local-01 (reddit), sig-local-02 (reddit), sig-local-03 (bluesky), sig-local-04 (reddit), sig-local-05 (bluesky)

steps.action: Verify threads in database for local radar
steps.observed: 5 threads confirmed: Llama/Release, Training/Analysis, Evaluation/Framework, Cloud/Spot, Open/Source. Threads have domain_tags like 'ai', 'open-source', 'community', 'deployment'.

steps.action: Verify API returns complete thread data
steps.expected: API response includes threads with source_distribution, domain_tags, members
steps.observed: API returns all 5 threads with complete data including members, source_distribution, domain_tags, status='emerging', confidence=0.5

steps.action: Check μ lane for thread cards with leverage/time-to-act indicators
steps.expected: Thread cards visible in μ lane
steps.observed: μ lane shows empty state: 'No local signals yet.' Zero thread cards rendered.

evidence.network: GET /api/radars -> 200 (includes Open Source AI Community radar with 5 threads)

assertions.issues: The signal→thread→API pipeline works correctly (signals collected, normalized, clustered into threads, served via API). The break occurs at the frontend routing layer: App.tsx filters tiles into lanes by radar category. The 'Open Source AI Community' radar has category='technology', but localTiles only accepts 'local'|'community'|'oss' categories. Consequently the radar (with its 5 threads) is routed to the Π (Connections) lane instead of μ (Local) lane. The MuLaneContent component receives an empty tiles array and renders the empty state. Fix: either add 'technology' to localTiles filter, or categorize the radar as 'local' or 'community'.

frictions.description: The radar category 'technology' is not recognized as a local/community category by the frontend localTiles filter (App.tsx line ~150). Only 'local', 'community', and 'oss' categories route to the μ lane. This is a data-UI contract mismatch that affects VAL-MU-002, VAL-MU-003, and VAL-CROSS-012.

Personalization weight dimension names ('geopolitical') don't exactly match data dimension names ('geopolitics' in Hormuz radar scoreRanges). Only 'infrastructure' matches exactly. This limits which gauges respond to slider changes.
VAL-CROSS-003 pass condition expects an API call when slider changes, but the implementation is purely client-side (localStorage + React state). No network request is made on slider change.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-MU-004, VAL-CROSS-003), 3 failed (VAL-MU-002, VAL-MU-003, VAL-CROSS-012). The primary failure cause is a category filter mismatch: the 'Open Source AI Community' radar has category='technology' but the frontend only routes 'local'/'community'/'oss' categories to the μ lane. This prevents all thread cards from rendering, causing VAL-MU-002 and VAL-CROSS-012 to fail. VAL-MU-003 also fails because MuThreadCard lacks an action suggestions feature. Community readiness gauges (VAL-MU-004) render correctly with progress bars and labels. Personalization slider (VAL-CROSS-003) correctly triggers re-render of matched dimension gauges.
